page_title: "signoz_rule Resource - signoz"
subcategory: ""
description: |-
  Creates and manages alert rules in SigNoz. Phase 2 supports a minimum-viable subset of the rule schema (METRIC_BASED_ALERT with a single promql query); the full schema is exposed but not yet wired through.
---

# signoz_rule (Resource)

Creates and manages alert rules in SigNoz.

~> **Phase 2 scope:** the resource accepts only this shape end-to-end —
> - `alert_type` = `METRIC_BASED_ALERT`
> - `rule_type` = `threshold_rule` or `promql_rule`
> - `condition.composite_query.query_type` = `promql`
> - exactly one entry in `condition.composite_query.queries[]`, of kind `promql`
> - `condition.thresholds` not set
> - `notification_settings` not set
>
> Other shapes (logs/traces/exceptions alerts, builder/clickhouse/formula queries, multi-step composites, threshold ladders, notification policies) are present in the schema for forward-compatibility but are rejected at apply time.

## Example Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    signoz = {
      source = "registry.terraform.io/signoz/signoz"
    }
  }
}

provider "signoz" {
  endpoint     = "http://localhost:8080"
  access_token = "<SIGNOZ-API-KEY>"
}

# Phase 2 minimum-viable rule:
#   alert_type = METRIC_BASED_ALERT
#   rule_type  = threshold_rule | promql_rule
#   query_type = promql, single query, no thresholds, no notification_settings
resource "signoz_rule" "high_error_rate" {
  alert       = "high-error-rate"
  alert_type  = "METRIC_BASED_ALERT"
  rule_type   = "promql_rule"
  version     = "v5"
  description = "Fires when 5xx rate over the last 5m exceeds 1 req/s."

  annotations = {
    summary     = "High error rate"
    description = "{{ $labels.service_name }} error rate is {{ $value }}"
  }

  labels = {
    severity = "warning"
  }

  condition = {
    composite_query = {
      panel_type = "graph"
      query_type = "promql"
      queries = [{
        promql = {
          name  = "A"
          query = "rate(http_requests_total{status=~\"5..\"}[5m])"
          step  = "60s"
        }
      }]
    }
    target     = 1
    op         = "above"
    match_type = "at_least_once"
  }

  evaluation = {
    rolling = {
      eval_window = "5m"
      frequency   = "1m"
    }
  }

  preferred_channels = ["platform-alerts-slack"]
}

output "rule_id" {
  value = signoz_rule.high_error_rate.id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->

## Schema

### Required

- `alert` (String) Name of the alert rule.
- `alert_type` (String) Alert family. Phase 2: must be `METRIC_BASED_ALERT`. Schema also accepts `LOGS_BASED_ALERT`, `TRACES_BASED_ALERT`, `EXCEPTIONS_BASED_ALERT` (rejected at apply).
- `condition` (Attributes) Alert condition: query, threshold, comparison op. (see below for nested schema)
- `rule_type` (String) Rule kind. Phase 2: `threshold_rule` or `promql_rule`.
- `version` (String) Rule schema version. Use `v5` for new rules.

### Optional

- `annotations` (Map of String) Rule annotations (e.g., `summary`, `description`). Templated against alert labels.
- `description` (String) Free-form description.
- `disabled` (Boolean) Disable the rule without deleting it.
- `eval_window` (String) Evaluation window as a Go duration. Equivalent to `evaluation.rolling.eval_window` for legacy rules.
- `evaluation` (Attributes) Evaluation strategy. Use `rolling` for sliding-window or `cumulative` for fixed-cadence. (see below for nested schema)
- `frequency` (String) Evaluation frequency. Equivalent to `evaluation.rolling.frequency` for legacy rules.
- `labels` (Map of String) Static labels attached to the alert.
- `notification_settings` (Attributes) ~> **Phase 2: not supported.** Configuring this field fails apply. (see below for nested schema)
- `preferred_channels` (List of String) Names of `signoz_notification_channel` resources to route this alert to.
- `schema_version` (String) Internal schema version. Defaults to a server-chosen value.
- `source` (String) Optional URL pointing back at the rule definition (e.g., a Git link).

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Autogenerated unique ID.
- `state` (String) Current alert state (e.g., `firing`, `inactive`).

### Nested Schema for `condition`

Required:

- `composite_query` (Attributes) The query graph evaluated for this rule. (see below for nested schema)

Optional:

- `absent_for` (Number) Seconds with no data before the rule fires `data_absent`.
- `alert_on_absent` (Boolean) Whether to fire when no data is returned.
- `algorithm` (String) Server-side algorithm tag.
- `match_type` (String) How the threshold is checked. One of: `at_least_once`, `all_the_times`, `on_average`, `in_total`, `last`.
- `op` (String) Comparison operator. One of: `above`, `below`, `equal`, `not_equal`, `outside_bounds`.
- `require_min_points` (Boolean), `required_num_points` (Number) Minimum sample-count gate before evaluating.
- `seasonality` (String) For anomaly rules: `hourly`, `daily`, `weekly`.
- `selected_query_name` (String) Name of the query in `composite_query.queries` whose result feeds the threshold.
- `target` (Number) Threshold value.
- `target_unit` (String) Unit for the threshold (informational).
- `thresholds` (Attributes) ~> **Phase 2: not supported.** Threshold ladders fail apply.

### Nested Schema for `condition.composite_query`

Required:

- `panel_type` (String) One of: `value`, `table`, `graph`.
- `queries` (Attributes List) Query steps. Each entry is a one-of of `builder_log` / `builder_metric` / `builder_trace` / `clickhouse_sql` / `formula` / `promql` / `trace_operator`. Phase 2: exactly one entry, kind `promql`.

Optional:

- `query_type` (String) Top-level query family. Phase 2: `promql`.

#### `composite_query.queries[].promql`

Optional:

- `name` (String) Step name (used as the result label).
- `query` (String) PromQL query.
- `step` (String) Step interval as a Go duration (e.g., `60s`).
- `disabled` (Boolean), `legend` (String).

Other step kinds (`builder_log`, `builder_metric`, `builder_trace`, `clickhouse_sql`, `formula`, `trace_operator`) carry their own attribute trees but are out of Phase 2 scope.

### Nested Schema for `evaluation`

Optional (exactly one of `rolling` or `cumulative` must be set):

- `rolling` (Attributes)
  - `eval_window` (String, Required) Sliding-window length (e.g., `5m`).
  - `frequency` (String, Required) Evaluation cadence (e.g., `1m`).
- `cumulative` (Attributes)
  - `frequency` (String, Required) Evaluation cadence.
  - `timezone` (String, Required) IANA timezone for boundaries.
  - `schedule` (Attributes, Required) Anchor schedule:
    - `type` (String, Required) One of: `hourly`, `daily`, `weekly`, `monthly`.
    - `minute` (Number), `hour` (Number), `weekday` (Number), `day` (Number) — selectors used by the chosen `type`.

### Nested Schema for `notification_settings`

~> Phase 2 rejects this attribute. Documented for forward-compatibility.

Optional:

- `group_by` (List of String) Labels to group notifications by.
- `new_group_eval_delay` (String) Delay before notifying on a brand-new group.
- `renotify` (Attributes)
  - `alert_states` (List of String) States that trigger renotify (e.g., `firing`, `nodata`).
  - `enabled` (Boolean), `interval` (String).
- `use_policy` (Boolean) Use route policies instead of `preferred_channels`.
